attractions.name: Basilica of Saint Louis, King of France (Old Cathedral)
attractions.description: The Basilica of Saint Louis, King of France, colloquially known as the Old Cathedral, is a historic Catholic church and the first cathedral built west of the Mississippi River. Located near the Gateway Arch, it serves as a personal parish and a major historical landmark, featuring a museum with artifacts from the early Catholic Church in St. Louis. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Basilica Open: Mon-Fri: 6:45 AM - 2:00 PM; Sat: 6:45 AM - 6:30 PM; Sun: 7:30 AM - 6:00 PM. Museum Hours (variable, typical schedule): Sun: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Mon: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Fri: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM. Official Site, Museum Calendar
attractions.admission: Free to visit the Basilica. Museum tours: Requested donation of $5 per person, $3 per student. First Sunday tours are free. Official Site
highlights: First cathedral west of the Mississippi River, with roots dating back to 1764. Official Site
Designated a minor basilica by Pope John XXIII in 1961. Wikipedia
Features a museum with a 1770 bell and artifacts from the early colonial west. Official Site
Greek Revival architecture with marble altars and notable religious paintings. Wikipedia
Located on the original 'Cathedral Block' designated at the city's founding. Official Site
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning for a peaceful experience or to attend daily Mass. The museum is located on the west side of the church and spans two floors; it is now ADA accessible. Official Site, See Sight Tours
attractions.nearby: Gateway Arch National Park, Gateway Arch, Mississippi Riverfront, Eads Bridge, Busch Stadium. Wikipedia, Explore St. Louis

attractions.name: Busch Stadium
attractions.description: Busch Stadium is a 44,000+ seat retro-style baseball stadium in downtown St. Louis and the home of the St. Louis Cardinals. Opened in 2006, the ballpark is famed for its lively atmosphere and spectacular views of the downtown skyline and the iconic Gateway Arch.
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Non-game days); Game Days: 10:00 AM (Mon-Fri) or 12:00 PM (Sat-Sun Night) until 1.5 hours after start. Team Store & Museum: Daily 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM. Stadium Gates: 1.5 hours before game (Mon-Thu), 2 hours before (Fri-Sun).
attractions.days_open: Daily for Team Store, Museum, and Box Office; Stadium gates open for scheduled games and events.
attractions.admission: Classic Tours: Adults $22, Seniors (60+)/Military $20, Children (15 & under) $18, Children 3 & under Free; Museum: Adults $12, Senior/Military $10, Child $8, 3 & under Free; Game tickets prices vary.
highlights: Panoramic views of the downtown St. Louis skyline and the Gateway Arch.
Home to the 11-time World Series Champion St. Louis Cardinals.
Adjacent to Ballpark Village, a massive 150,000-square-foot entertainment and dining district.
Features the Cardinals Hall of Fame and Museum, housing one of the largest team-specific collections in MLB.
Iconic Gate 3 entrance featuring a 'bridge' design inspired by the Eads Bridge and a bronze statue of Stan Musial.
Modern 'retro-classic' architecture with high-definition video boards and the Budweiser Terrace social area.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to visit the many player statues and Ballpark Village before the game. The stadium is entirely cashless, so ensure you have a card for all concession and retail purchases. Use the MetroLink light rail (8th Street station) to avoid heavy game-day traffic and parking fees. Be sure to check the bag policy, as large bags and certain containers are prohibited.
attractions.nearby: Ballpark Village, Gateway Arch, Old Courthouse, National Blues Museum, City Museum, Laclede's Landing.

attractions.name: Enterprise Center (formerly Scottrade Center)
attractions.description: Enterprise Center is a premier multi-purpose arena located in downtown St. Louis, Missouri. It serves as the home ice for the NHL's St. Louis Blues and hosts a wide array of professional sports, concerts, and family shows throughout the year. Enterprise Center About
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday-Friday 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; open later and on weekends for event days. Enterprise Center Contact
attractions.days_open: Varies by event schedule; Box Office typically open Monday-Friday and on event days. Enterprise Center Schedule
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; tickets are primarily available through Ticketmaster and the venue box office. Enterprise Center Tickets
highlights: Home of the NHL St. Louis Blues hockey team.
Hosts major concerts, professional wrestling, and family entertainment.
Features premium seating options including suites and theater boxes.
Located in the heart of downtown St. Louis.
Renovated facility with modern amenities and fan experiences.
attractions.tips: Check the bag policy and arena guidelines before visiting; use the MetroLink for easy access to the venue. Enterprise Center Arena Policies
attractions.nearby: Stifel Theatre, St. Louis Union Station, Soldiers Memorial Military Museum. Enterprise Center Visit

attractions.name: Field House Museum (formerly Eugene Field House & St. Louis Toy Museum) (Wikipedia))
attractions.description: The Field House Museum is a historic house museum in St. Louis that was the childhood home of Eugene Field, known as the 'Children's Poet,' and the residence of his father, Roswell Field. Roswell Field was a prominent attorney who formulated the legal strategy for the landmark Dred Scott freedom suit, making the site a National Historic Landmark. Today, the museum features original family furnishings, an extensive antique toy collection, and rotating exhibits in its modern wing addition. (Wikipedia)), (Field House Museum)
attractions.address: 634 South Broadway, St. Louis, Missouri 63102 (Field House Museum)
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM. Closed Monday, Tuesday, and major holidays. (Field House Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00, AAA Members: $9.00, Children 7–16: $5.00, Children 6 & under: Free. St. Louis City public school groups are admitted free (Field House Museum), (Idealist)
highlights: Childhood home and birthplace of Eugene Field, the "Children\
attractions.tips: Guided tours are highly recommended for detailed historical context; there is a free parking lot available for visitors behind the museum, and you may need to ring the doorbell to be admitted. Be prepared to navigate three flights of stairs in the historic house section. (Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Busch Stadium, Gateway Arch National Park, Old Courthouse, Broadway Oyster Bar, Cardinals Hall of Fame and Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Gateway Arch National Park
attractions.description: Gateway Arch National Park is an iconic American landmark that commemorates St. Louis’ role in the 19th-century westward expansion of the United States. The park features the world's tallest man-made monument, a world-class museum, and the historic Old Courthouse, the site of the pivotal Dred Scott civil rights case.
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: 9:00am – 6:00pm daily (Extended summer hours May 22 – Sep 7: 9:00am – 8:00pm Fri-Sat); Park Grounds: 5:00am – 11:00pm daily; Old Courthouse: 9:00am – 5:00pm daily
attractions.days_open: Daily (Year-round except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year’s Day)
attractions.admission: Tram Ride to the Top: Adult (16+) $15–$19, Child (3–15) $11–$15; Documentary Movie: Adult $7, Child $3; Museum and Park Grounds: Free
highlights: Tram ride to the observation deck at the top of the 630-foot Gateway Arch
The Museum under the Gateway Arch detailing 200 years of shared history
The historic Old Courthouse (site of the Dred Scott decision)
St. Louis Riverfront cruises on authentic paddlewheel riverboats
91 acres of scenic park grounds for walking and biking along the Mississippi River
'Monument to the Dream' documentary film showcasing the Arch's construction
attractions.tips: Book tram tickets in advance as they often sell out early; allow at least 30 minutes for security screening; the museum is free to the public even without a tram ticket.
attractions.nearby: Old Cathedral, Busch Stadium, National Blues Museum, Citygarden Sculpture Park, St. Louis Union Station

attractions.name: Old Courthouse (Old St. Louis County Courthouse)
attractions.description: The Old Courthouse is a historic landmark and museum that served as the site of the pivotal Dred Scott freedom trials and Virginia Minor's women's suffrage case. Recently reopened in May 2025 after an extensive renovation, the building features four new museum galleries and restored historic courtrooms beneath its iconic Renaissance dome (National Park Service, Gateway Arch Park Foundation).
attractions.hours: Mon: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tue: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wed: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thu: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Fri: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sat: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sun: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily (closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: Site of the landmark Dred Scott trials in 1847 and 1850 which played a key role in the lead-up to the Civil War (National Park Service)
Location where Virginia Minor's 1872 case for a woman's right to vote came to trial (National Park Service)
Member of the National Park Service's National Underground Railroad Network to Freedom (National Park Service)
Stunning 19th-century Greek Revival architecture featuring a high-profile Renaissance dome (Explore St. Louis)
Four innovative new exhibit galleries opened in 2025 focusing on the judicial system and civil rights history (The Gateway Arch)
Two historically furnished courtrooms restored to their mid-19th-century appearance (National Park Service)
attractions.tips: National Park Service rangers provide free guided tours of the building daily at 2:15 PM. Pre-purchasing parking at the nearby Stadium East Parking Garage via iparkit.com/TheArch is highly recommended for a discounted rate. The building is fully accessible with new elevators and ramps following the 2025 renovation (National Park Service).
attractions.nearby: Gateway Arch, Old Cathedral, Kiener Plaza, Luther Ely Smith Square, Stadium East Parking Garage (National Park Service, The Gateway Arch)

attractions.name: Stifel Theatre (formerly Peabody Opera House)
attractions.description: Stifel Theatre is a historic 3,100-seat civic performing arts building located in the heart of downtown St. Louis. Originally opened in 1934 as the Kiel Opera House, the venue underwent a massive renovation and reopened in 2011 to host concerts, Broadway shows, comedy performances, and corporate events (Stifel Theatre).
attractions.hours: Weekdays: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Hotlines); Event Days: Noon - ~1 hour after start (Stifel Theatre)
attractions.days_open: Weekdays (administration/hotlines); Event days as scheduled (Stifel Theatre)
attractions.admission: Varies by event (tiers available on Ticketmaster); Tours: $10/person (groups 10-19), $8/person (groups 20+) (Stifel Theatre, Ticketmaster)
highlights: Historic 3,100-seat main theatre with world-class acoustics (Tripadvisor)
Grand two-story front lobby constructed entirely of Tennessee and Ste. Genevieve marble (Stifel Theatre)
Features seven distinct venues, including four smaller side halls with up to 700 seats each (Stifel Theatre)
Reopened in 2011 following a comprehensive $78.7 million restoration (Stifel Theatre)
Part of a significant grouping of civic buildings along the Memorial Plaza (Stifel Theatre)
attractions.tips: The venue is cash-free for all concessions and merchandise; use of SpotHero is recommended to prepay for parking during events (Stifel Theatre, Stifel Theatre). Accessible entry is available at the corner of 15th and Market (MindsEye Radio).
attractions.nearby: Enterprise Center, St. Louis City Hall, Soldiers Memorial Military Museum, Central Public Library, Washington Avenue (Stifel Theatre, Reddit)

attractions.name: Forest Park
attractions.description: Forest Park is a 1,300-acre urban oasis in St. Louis, known as one of the most beautiful urban parks in the world and larger than New York's Central Park. It features a diverse landscape of woodlands, waterways, and prairies, and serves as the city's civic heart, hosting world-class cultural institutions and major community events.
attractions.region: Western St. Louis (bordered by Central West End and Forest Park Southeast)
attractions.hours: Park: 6:00 a.m. – 10:00 p.m. daily; Visitor Center (Warm Weather): Mon-Fri 6:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m., Sat-Sun 6:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m.; Visitor Center (Cold Weather): Mon-Fri 6:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m., Sat-Sun 6:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.
attractions.admission: Free (General admission for the park and its major museums: Saint Louis Art Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, Missouri History Museum, and Saint Louis Zoo. Some special exhibitions and the Jewel Box conservatory may have a small fee.)
highlights: Saint Louis Art Museum
Saint Louis Zoo
Saint Louis Science Center & Planetarium
The Muny (outdoor musical theatre)
Missouri History Museum
The Jewel Box conservatory
Anne O’C. Albrecht Nature Playscape
attractions.tips: General admission to the park and its major museums is free, making it one of the most accessible cultural destinations in the U.S. Use the interactive map on the official website to navigate the 30 miles of paths, and consider visiting Art Hill for picnics in summer or sledding in winter.
attractions.nearby: Central West End, Forest Park Southeast (The Grove), Washington University in St. Louis, Saint Louis University

attractions.name: Jefferson Lake (Forest Park)
attractions.description: Jefferson Lake is a premier fishing destination in Forest Park, spanning nine acres and featuring a diverse ecosystem stocked with bass, catfish, and rainbow trout. Recently revitalized through the East Waterways Project, it now boasts a cascading waterfall, an ADA-accessible boardwalk, and a scenic overlook. It serves as a key recreational "chill spot" on the eastern edge of the park, offering a peaceful atmosphere for anglers and visitors alike.
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Premier fishing lake stocked with bass, catfish, crappie, carp, and sunfish
Winter trout fishing destination stocked annually with rainbow trout (November-February)
Bayer Boardwalk, an ADA-accessible path providing direct water-edge access
Wise Falls, a stunning 1,300-ton boulder cascading waterfall at the south end
Interconnected waterway system linked to the Taylor Kindle River and Bowl Lake
Scenic overlook and donor plaza at Clayton Avenue for birdwatching and relaxation
attractions.tips: A valid Missouri fishing license is required for all anglers between ages 16 and 65. For winter trout season (Nov 1 - Jan 31), fishing is catch-and-release only unless you have a trout permit for harvesting. Check the fish stocking hotline for the most recent updates on trout and catfish releases. The new Bayer Boardwalk offers the best accessible spots for casting.
attractions.nearby: Steinberg Ice Skating Rink, Bowl Lake, Seven Pools, Round Lake, Saint Louis Zoo, Saint Louis Art Museum

attractions.name: Missouri History Museum
attractions.description: The Missouri History Museum in St. Louis' Forest Park showcases the city's storied past, from its founding in 1764 to the 1904 World’s Fair and beyond. Housed in the historic 1913 Jefferson Memorial Building, the museum offers a variety of permanent and rotating exhibits, including a dedicated interactive space for children called the History Clubhouse. It serves as a central hub for regional history and community events like the Twilight Thursdays concert series. (Missouri Historical Society, Visit Missouri)
attractions.hours: Monday: CLOSED; Tuesday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Wednesday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Thursday: 10:00am–8:00pm; Friday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Saturday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Sunday: 10:00am–5:00pm. (Missouri History Museum)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Generally closed on Mondays except for select holidays) (Missouri History Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (General Admission); Some special exhibitions may require a fee; Children 5 and under are free for all exhibits. (Missouri Historical Society, Explore St. Louis)
highlights: 1904 World’s Fair extensive collection and exhibits (Explore St. Louis)
Charles Lindbergh's Spirit of St. Louis artifacts (Wikipedia)
History Clubhouse interactive gallery for children and families (Missouri History Museum)
Mill Creek: Black Metropolis special exhibition (Missouri Historical Society)
Twilight Thursdays outdoor concert series (Missouri History Museum)
See STL guided walking and bus tours of St. Louis neighborhoods (Explore St. Louis)
attractions.tips: The museum offers extended hours on Thursdays until 8:00pm, making it a great time for evening visits. Note that the History Clubhouse closes earlier at 4:00pm daily, regardless of the museum's overall hours. Free parking is available in the museum's twin lots and along the streets of Forest Park. (Missouri History Museum, Expedia)
attractions.nearby: St. Louis Art Museum, St. Louis Zoo, Saint Louis Science Center, The Muny, and Forest Park's various trails and lakes (Explore St. Louis, Visit Missouri)

attractions.name: Missouri History Museum (1904 World's Fair Exhibit)
attractions.description: The 1904 World's Fair exhibit at the Missouri History Museum offers a comprehensive look at the Louisiana Purchase Exposition, featuring over 200 artifacts and a massive scale model of the fairgrounds. It explores both the grandeur of the event and its complex legacy, including the impacts on diverse cultures and the technological innovations showcased at the time.
attractions.hours: Monday: CLOSED; Tuesday: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Wednesday: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Thursday: 10:00am - 8:00pm; Friday: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Saturday: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Sunday: 10:00am - 5:00pm (Missouri History Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri History Museum)
highlights: Massive 17-by-25-foot scale model of the 1904 fairgrounds showing over 1,500 buildings
Over 200 artifacts including original furniture, souvenirs, and architectural models from the fair
Immersive 3D stereograph displays and hundreds of historical photos
Exhibits detailing the fair\
human zoo
Meet Me in St. Louis
attractions.tips: Visit on Thursday evenings for extended hours until 8:00 pm. Arrive early at 10:00 am to beat the crowds at the popular History Clubhouse kids' area, and consider the guided World's Fair gallery tours offered on Thursday and Sunday afternoons (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Art Museum, Saint Louis Zoo, Forest Park, World's Fair Pavilion, Grand Basin and Art Hill (Explore St. Louis)

attractions.name: Saint Louis Art Museum (SLAM)
attractions.description: The Saint Louis Art Museum is a world-renowned institution featuring a comprehensive collection of over 37,000 objects spanning 5,000 years of history and cultures (SLAM). Housed in a landmark building from the 1904 World's Fair, it serves as a central cultural hub in the heart of Forest Park (Wikipedia).
attractions.address: One Fine Arts Drive, Forest Park, St. Louis, Missouri 63110 (SLAM)
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Sunday: 10 am–5 pm; Friday: 10 am–9 pm; Closed Monday (SLAM)
attractions.admission: Admission to the Museum is free every day. Admission to special exhibitions is free on Fridays (SLAM).
highlights: Comprehensive collection of over 37,000 objects spanning 5,000 years of history (SLAM)
The world's largest collection of paintings by German artist Max Beckmann (Wikipedia)
The Grace Taylor Broughton Sculpture Garden, featuring outdoor modern and contemporary works (SLAM)
Iconic artworks such as Edgar Degas\
Little Dancer of 14 Years
attractions.tips: Visit on Fridays for extended evening hours until 9 pm and free admission to special exhibitions (SLAM). Free public parking is available in the lots on the north side of Fine Arts Drive (SLAM).
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Zoo, Missouri History Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, and Art Hill in Forest Park (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Saint Louis Science Center Saint Louis Science Center
attractions.category: Science Museum and Planetarium Saint Louis Science Center - Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Saint Louis Science Center is a collection of buildings including a science museum and planetarium located on the southeastern corner of Forest Park. Its mission is to inspire everyone to be curious and engaged in science through more than 700 interactive experiences and a world-class OMNIMAX Theater. Saint Louis Science Center - Wikipedia, Saint Louis Science Center About Us
attractions.address: 5050 Oakland Avenue, St. Louis, MO 63110 Saint Louis Science Center
attractions.city: St. Louis Saint Louis Science Center
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Tuesday: CLOSED; Wednesday: CLOSED; Thursday: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Friday: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Saint Louis Science Center Hours
attractions.days_open: Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesday and Wednesday; Open for 6th day starting June 3, 2026). Saint Louis Science Center Hours
attractions.admission: General admission is free. Ticketed attractions: OMNIMAX Theater ($11.50 adults, $9.50 children/seniors); Discovery Room ($6 for non-members, free for members); Experience Flight Simulators ($6–$10 non-members). Saint Louis Science Center FAQs, OMNIMAX Theater, Discovery Room, Flight Simulators
highlights: James S. McDonnell Planetarium with its distinctive hyperbolic paraboloid shape Saint Louis Science Center
OMNIMAX Theater featuring a four-story IMAX Dome with Laser Saint Louis Science Center
GROW, an interactive permanent exhibition dedicated to agriculture and farming Saint Louis Science Center
GameXPloration, a gallery exploring the science and technology of gaming Saint Louis Science Center
Life Science Lab where visitors can engage in hands-on experiments Saint Louis Science Center
McDonnell Planetarium's genuine space capsules from the Mercury and Gemini programs Saint Louis Science Center
attractions.tips: General admission is free, but some attractions like the OMNIMAX Theater and Discovery Room require paid tickets. Parking at the Oakland building lot costs $15, but free parking may be found near the Planetarium entrance in Forest Park. Saint Louis Science Center FAQs
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Zoo, Saint Louis Art Museum, Missouri History Museum, The Muny, and Forest Park Forest Park Forever

attractions.name: Saint Louis Zoological Park (Saint Louis Zoo)
attractions.description: The Saint Louis Zoological Park is a leading zoo located in Forest Park, recognized for its excellence in animal management, research, conservation, and education. It houses more than 18,700 animals representing over 600 species and is consistently ranked among the top zoos in the United States. Saint Louis Zoo Mission, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Feb 28 - May 21: 9 a.m.–5 p.m. daily; May 22-25: 8 a.m.–6 p.m.; May 26 - Aug 23: 8 a.m.–5 p.m. (Sun-Thu) & 8 a.m.–6 p.m. (Fri-Sat); Aug 24 - Sept 4: 9 a.m.–5 p.m. daily; Sept 5-7: 8 a.m.–6 p.m.; Sept 8 - Oct 7: 9 a.m.–5 p.m. daily; Oct 8 - Dec 31: 9 a.m.–4 p.m. daily (Closed Dec 25) Saint Louis Zoo
attractions.days_open: Open daily (Closed on Christmas Day and New Year's Day) Saint Louis Zoo
attractions.admission: Free (fees apply for some special attractions like the 4D Theater, Conservation Carousel, and Sea Lion Show) Saint Louis Zoo
highlights: Penguin and Puffin Coast with walk-through exhibits Saint Louis Zoo
Sea Lion Sound featuring an underwater viewing tunnel Saint Louis Zoo
River's Edge multispecies immersion exhibits Saint Louis Zoo
Emerson Zooline Railroad narrow-gauge train Saint Louis Zoo
Bayer Insectarium and Butterfly House Saint Louis Zoo
Centene Grizzly Ridge state-of-the-art habitat Saint Louis Zoo
attractions.tips: Admission is free, but consider an Adventure Pass for access to multiple paid attractions. Arrive early to secure parking, which is $20 for non-members in zoo lots, or look for free street parking within Forest Park. Use the STL Zoo digital map for real-time navigation and animal information. Saint Louis Zoo Parking
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Art Museum, Missouri History Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, The Jewel Box, Missouri Botanical Garden Wikipedia, Explore St. Louis

attractions.name: St. Louis Municipal Opera Theatre (The Muny)
attractions.description: The Muny is America's oldest and largest outdoor musical theatre, producing a summer season of Broadway-quality musicals in the heart of Forest Park. Founded in 1919, the 11,000-seat amphitheater is a cherished St. Louis tradition known for its commitment to accessibility and high production standards under the stars.
attractions.hours: Shows start at 8:15 p.m. nightly during the performance season; Box Office: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. Monday – Friday. Muny FAQ, Explore St. Louis
attractions.days_open: Monday through Sunday during the summer season (typically mid-June to mid-August); Box Office is open Monday-Friday year-round.
attractions.admission: Single tickets start at $20; Free seats (last nine rows, approx. 1,500 seats) are available on a first-come, first-served basis; Season tickets start at $126 for seven shows. Muny Tickets, Ticket Savings
highlights: Produces seven major musical theater productions each summer.
Features approximately 1,500 free seats available for every performance.
Tony Award-winning regional theatre for artistic achievement.
Hosts the 'Muny Magic at The Sheldon' concert series.
Historic 11.5-acre campus with a silent overhead cooling system for summer nights.
Offers educational programs like Muny Kids & Teens and Technical Theatre Training.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to claim free seats as gates open at 7 p.m.; bring your own food and water (no glass or alcohol in the theatre); dress casually for the summer heat; and use the free courtesy shuttle within Forest Park. Muny FAQ, St. Louis Mom
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Zoo, Saint Louis Art Museum, Missouri History Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, Jewel Box, The Boathouse. Forest Park Forever

attractions.name: The Jewel Box (St. Louis Floral Conservatory)
attractions.description: The Jewel Box is a historic Art Deco display greenhouse and floral conservatory located on a 17-acre site in Forest Park. Built in 1936, it features unconventional cantilevered glass walls rising 50 feet high and houses permanent floral displays supplemented by seasonal shows.
attractions.address: 1 Wells Drive, McKinley Dr Forest Park
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 9 a.m. to 4 p.m.; Friday: 9 a.m. to 3 p.m.; Saturday: 9 a.m. to 11 a.m.; Sunday: 9 a.m. to 2 p.m.
attractions.admission: $1 per person (Free on Monday and Tuesday from 9 a.m. until noon)
highlights: Art Deco architecture with 50-foot high glass walls (Wikipedia))
National Register of Historic Places listing (City of St. Louis)
Seasonal floral shows including Christmas, Easter, and Mother's Day (City of St. Louis)
Interior fountain and water feature (City of St. Louis)
Surrounded by formal gardens and reflecting pools (Wikipedia))
Popular venue for weddings and special events (Forest Park Forever)
attractions.tips: Visit on Monday or Tuesday mornings before noon for free admission. The venue is often rented for weddings on weekends, so public hours are limited. (City of St. Louis)
attractions.nearby: St. Louis Art Museum, St. Louis Zoo, Missouri History Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, The Muny (Missouri Division of Tourism)

attractions.name: Urban Chestnut Brewing Company
attractions.description: Urban Chestnut Brewing Company is an unconventional-minded yet tradition-oriented craft brewer known for its unique 'Beer Divergency' philosophy. The company operates two distinct St. Louis locations: a large industrial German-style Bierhall in The Grove and a traditional Biergarten in Midtown, both offering a wide selection of authentic lagers and ales alongside European-inspired fare.
attractions.address: 4465 Manchester Avenue (Grove Bierhall); 3229 Washington Avenue (Midtown Biergarten)
attractions.region: The Grove (Forest Park Southeast) and Grand Center (Covenant Blu)
attractions.hours: Grove Bierhall: Mon-Wed: 3pm-10pm, Thurs: 11am-10pm, Fri-Sat: 11am-12am, Sun: 11am-9pm. Midtown Biergarten: Wed-Thurs: 4pm-10pm, Fri-Sat: 12pm-10pm, Sun: 12pm-7pm.
attractions.admission: Free (General Entry); Saturday Brewery Tours: $12.00 (includes a beer)
highlights: Traditional German-style Bierhall and Biergarten experiences
Extensive selection of craft beers including the flagship Zwickel lager
Partnership with Fordo's Killer Pizza for food at the Grove location
Saturday afternoon guided brewery tours for a behind-the-scenes look
Dog-friendly outdoor patios and terraces at both city locations
Commitment to sustainability through their 'Urban Efforts' program
attractions.tips: The Grove Bierhall is better for large groups and communal dining, while the Midtown Biergarten offers a more intimate and traditional outdoor experience. No reservations are taken, so call ahead for large parties. Check their weekly tap list online as beer offerings change frequently.
attractions.nearby: The Monocle, Tower Grove Park, Saint Louis Science Center, Missouri Botanical Garden, Fox Theatre (near Midtown)

attractions.name: World's Fair Pavilion
attractions.description: The World's Fair Pavilion is a magnificent open-air shelter built in 1909 with proceeds from the 1904 Louisiana Purchase Exposition. Sitting high atop Government Hill, it commands a dramatic view of Forest Park and serves as a premier location for picturesque private events and public gatherings City of St. Louis.
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Standard Park Hours) Forest Park Forever
attractions.admission: Free for general public access; Private rental fees range from $1,475 to $4,200 depending on the day and duration City of St. Louis
highlights: Built in 1909 on the original site of the 1904 World's Fair Missouri State Building Wikipedia)
Designed by renowned English architect Henry Wright in the Mission Revival style Wikipedia)
Offers a 5,500-square-foot floor space capable of hosting up to 500 guests City of St. Louis
Features a dramatic overlook with stunning views of the park's landscape and nearby fountains City of St. Louis
Underwent a $1.1 million renovation in 1998 to add modern catering and restroom facilities City of St. Louis
attractions.tips: Reservations for private events must be made in person at the Parks Division office. It is a highly popular spot for wedding photography due to its elevated views and historic architecture. Public access is limited during private permitted events City of St. Louis.
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Zoo, Saint Louis Art Museum, The Jewel Box, The Muny, and The Cascades waterfall Forest Park Forever

attractions.name: Bowood Farms

This family-owned destination features a wide selection of plants, garden supplies, and home goods, complemented by an on-site restaurant and a creative workshop space (About Bowood).

attractions.name: Cathedral Basilica of Saint Louis Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis
attractions.description: The Cathedral Basilica of Saint Louis is a premier architectural landmark in the Central West End neighborhood, known for its masterful blend of Byzantine and Romanesque styles. It features one of the largest and most intricate collections of mosaics in the world, consisting of over 41 million glass pieces that depict biblical scenes and local history Wikipedia. Designated a basilica by Pope John Paul II in 1997, it serves as the mother church of the Archdiocese of St. Louis and a place of profound artistic and spiritual significance Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis.

attractions.city: St. Louis Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis

attractions.days_open: Daily Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis
attractions.admission: Cathedral: Free (Donations welcome); Mosaic Museum: $2 suggested donation; Guided Tours: $2 suggested donation Tripadvisor, Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis

Unique Byzantine Revival interior and Romanesque Revival exterior architecture Wikipedia
Lower-level Mosaic Museum showcasing the history and craftsmanship of the installations Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis
Burial crypts of former Archbishops and Cardinals of St. Louis Wikipedia
The Angel of Harmony sculpture, promoting peace and racial justice on the church grounds Wikipedia
Historic Kilgen organ console and artifacts from the 1999 visit of Pope John Paul II Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to wait until Mass is completed before touring the interior. Free parking is available in the lots located behind the Cathedral. For those interested in the mosaics, the lower-level museum is highly recommended for context on their 80-year creation process Cathedral Basilica of St. Louis, Tripadvisor.

attractions.name: Central West End
attractions.description: St. Louis’ Central West End is a bustling mixed-use district located on the eastern edge of Forest Park. Born from the energy of the 1904 World’s Fair, it is a vibrant neighborhood rich in turn-of-the-century architecture, culture, and community spirit. It is highly walkable and offers a variety of amenities including eclectic shops, galleries, and world-class dining. (Central West End Scene, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public Neighborhood); Shop and restaurant hours vary by establishment (Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel, Washington University in St. Louis)
highlights: Cathedral Basilica of Saint Louis, home to one of the world's largest mosaic collections (Expedia)
World Chess Hall of Fame, featuring the world's largest chess piece (Airial Travel)
Euclid Avenue, the district's main commercial corridor for shopping and dining (Wikipedia)
Chase Park Plaza, a historic landmark hotel and entertainment hub (Medical Center Redevelopment Corporation)
Direct access to Forest Park and its free cultural institutions (Wikipedia)
Historic private streets like Portland and Westmoreland Places (City of St. Louis)
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is highly walkable; comfortable shoes are recommended. Street parking is available but limited; utilize local parking garages or the MetroLink (CWE Station) for easier access. Arrive early on weekends when the area is most active. (Airial Travel)

Recently renovated and reopened, it now features an on-site brewery, Rock & Horse Brewing Company, and remains a beloved cultural landmark adorned with portraits of authors and musicians (Official Site, St. Louis Magazine).

attractions.name: Pi Pizzeria (Central West End) (St. Louis Magazine)
attractions.description: Pi Pizzeria is a renowned St. Louis establishment famous for its signature cornmeal crust and deep-dish pizzas, which gained national fame as a favorite of former President Barack Obama. The restaurant offered a unique alternative to traditional St. Louis-style pizza by prohibiting Provel cheese and focusing on high-quality ingredients and sustainable, 'green' dining practices (Sauce Magazine, St. Louis Magazine).

Strict policy against using Provel cheese, differentiating it from local St. Louis-style pizza (YouTube)
Certified 'Green' restaurant focusing on sustainability (YouTube)

attractions.tips: Although the physical locations are closed, the recipes and brand were put up for sale in 2024; fans should keep an eye out for potential re-openings or pop-up events by the original founders (St. Louis Magazine). Historically, the 'Bucktown' and 'Delmar' pizzas were the most recommended signature pies (Experience FoodBoss).

attractions.name: Anheuser-Busch St. Louis Brewery Experience

Visitors can explore the historic campus, visit the iconic Budweiser Clydesdales, and learn about the company's heritage while sampling fresh beer directly from the source. attractions.description: Soulard is a historic and vibrant neighborhood in St. Louis, known as the city's oldest still-intact community. It is famous for its unique brick row houses, a lively nightlife scene featuring blues music clubs and taverns, and the historic Soulard Farmers Market (Wikipedia, Soulard Restoration Group).

attractions.name: Pappy's Smokehouse
attractions.description: Pappy's Smokehouse is a nationally acclaimed barbecue restaurant located in the Midtown area of St. Louis, Missouri. Established in 2008, it is famous for its Memphis-style dry-rubbed ribs that are slow-smoked for up to 24 hours over apple and cherry wood. The restaurant has been named the best rib joint in America by The Food Network and continues to be a local tradition with a dedicated following. (Pappy's Smokehouse, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Saint Louis University
attractions.description: Saint Louis University (SLU) is a private Jesuit research university founded in 1818, making it the oldest university west of the Mississippi River. It is deeply committed to faith, service, and academic excellence, offering a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs across its urban Midtown campus and a second campus in Madrid, Spain (SLU About, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: St. Louis Symphony Orchestra
attractions.description: The St. Louis Symphony Orchestra, founded in 1880, is the second-oldest professional symphony orchestra in the United States and a nine-time Grammy Award winner. It performs a diverse repertoire ranging from classical masterpieces to live film scores at the historic, newly renovated Powell Hall within the Jack C. Taylor Music Center. Under the leadership of Music Director Stéphane Denève, the SLSO continues to be a cornerstone of the St. Louis arts community Wikipedia, St. Louis Symphony Orchestra

After a meticulous restoration in 1982, it now hosts a wide array of Broadway touring productions, concerts, and comedy shows in its opulent, Siamese-Byzantine-styled auditorium Fabulous Fox Theatre, City of St. Louis

attractions.name: Benton Park Neighborhood
attractions.description: Benton Park is a historic south St. Louis neighborhood renowned for its remarkably preserved 19th-century red brick architecture and its namesake 14.3-acre park. The area is a vibrant community hub featuring a diverse array of local eateries, the famous Cherokee Antique Row, and a rich history tied to the city's early brewing industry and former cemeteries. (City of St. Louis, Dawn Griffin Real Estate Group)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM (City of St. Louis)
attractions.admission: Free (YouTube)
attractions.phone: 314-622-4800 (City Services), 314-657-1363 (Neighborhood Specialist) (City of St. Louis, BPNA)
highlights: Historic 19th-century red brick architecture and Victorian-style row houses (YouTube)
The namesake Benton Park featuring a lake, rustic stone bridge, and playground (City of St. Louis)
Cherokee Antique Row, a premier destination for vintage and antique shopping (Dawn Griffin Real Estate Group)
Proximity to historic brewery sites and underground cave networks like the Lemp Brewery (City of St. Louis)
Vibrant dining scene including local favorites like Blues City Deli and Sidney Street Cafe (Tripadvisor)
Active community life with free summer concerts and local festivals (BPNA)
attractions.tips: Visit during the summer to enjoy free outdoor concerts in the park, and explore Cherokee Street on foot to fully appreciate the unique shops and street art. For popular dining spots like Blues City Deli, try to arrive before or after the peak lunch hour to avoid long lines. (Dawn Griffin Real Estate Group, Tripadvisor)

attractions.category: Landmark, Museum, State Historic Site, UNESCO World Heritage Site
attractions.description: Cahokia Mounds is the largest pre-Columbian settlement north of Mexico, preserving the archaeological remains of the Mississippian culture's most influential urban center. At its peak around 1100 CE, the city covered over 4,000 acres and included 120 earthen mounds with a population of up to 20,000 people. UNESCO, Official Website
attractions.region: St. Louis Metro East / St. Clair County
attractions.hours: Grounds: Daily, Dawn to Dusk. Interpretive Center/Museum: Currently closed for mechanical renovations; check official site for updates. Official Website

attractions.admission: Free; suggested donation of $7 for adults, $4 for children, and $15 for families. Wikivoyage, Official Website
highlights: Monks Mound (the largest prehistoric earthen structure in the Americas)
Woodhenge (an ancient solar calendar reconstructed on-site)
Mound 72 (a significant archaeological site with elite burials)
Grand Plaza (the central ceremonial heart of the ancient city)
Reconstructed Palisade (defensive stockade wall)
Interpretive Center (featuring world-class exhibits and artifacts)
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for climbing Monks Mound and dress for the weather as most of the site is outdoors. Check the official website or Facebook page for the latest status on Interpretive Center renovations before visiting. Official Website

attractions.name: Carondelet Park
attractions.description: Carondelet Park is St. Louis' third-largest park, covering approximately 180 acres of varied topography, including rolling hills and unique sinkholes. Established in 1875, it serves as a historic scenic retreat featuring a picturesque boathouse, fishing lakes, and extensive recreational facilities. (City of St. Louis, Friends of Carondelet Park)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (thatawaydad.com)
attractions.admission: Free (Park entry); Carondelet Park Rec Complex Outdoor Aquatic Center: Youth $9, Adults $13, Seniors $11, Households $34 (YMCA)

Historic Lyle House (circa 1850) and 1918 Boathouse

1.6-mile bicycle path and connection to the River des Peres Greenway
Unique landscape features such as natural sinkholes and a "White Wall" history
attractions.tips: St. Louis residents can pick up free mulch and compost at the city's operation within the park. The park is a popular spot for birdwatching near the shrubs and grasses by the trail intersections. (thatawaydad.com)

attractions.name: Cherokee Street (Cherokee Street Community Improvement District)
attractions.description: Cherokee Street is a vibrant center of commerce, culture, and creativity in South St. Louis, serving as a hub for galleries, antiques, and independent businesses. It is home to a significant Latine community and is renowned for its diverse culinary scene, historic architecture, and major cultural festivals. (Cherokee Street, Wikipedia))
attractions.region: South City (Benton Park, Benton Park West, Gravois Park, Marine Villa) (Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Individual business hours typically 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM, bars open late) (Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (Explore St. Louis)
highlights: Cherokee Antique Row: A six-block stretch of unique antique and vintage shops between Jefferson and Lemp Avenues. (Explore St. Louis)
Calle Cherokee: A high concentration of authentic Hispanic-owned bakeries, restaurants, and groceries. (Cherokee Street)
Cinco de Mayo: Hosts the largest Cinco de Mayo celebration in St. Louis along with the People's Joy Parade. (Wikipedia))
Arts Scene: Numerous galleries, creative incubators, and studios like The Luminary and Firecracker Press. (Cherokee Street)
Brewery History: Located near historic brewery sites and home to craft breweries like Bluewood Brewing and Earthbound Beer. (Wikipedia))
Events: Signature events including the Cherokee Print Bazaar, Jazz Crawl, and Brewed Arts Festival. (Explore St. Louis)
attractions.tips: Visit on a Saturday to explore the most open shops on Antique Row, and be sure to try the tacos at Taqueria El Bronco or La Vallesana. Check the community calendar for local events and live music crawls. (Explore St. Louis, Reddit)

attractions.name: Chesterfield Valley
attractions.description: Chesterfield Valley, historically known as \"Gumbo Flats,\" is a expansive retail, industrial, and entertainment corridor located in the Missouri River floodplain. Following the Great Flood of 1993, the area underwent a massive transformation protected by a multi-billion dollar levee system and now serves as one of the Midwest's most valuable retail hubs. Wikipedia, Explore St. Louis

attractions.description: Established in 1853, Kirkwood was the first planned residential commuter suburb west of the Mississippi River, named after James Pugh Kirkwood. This vibrant community in Southwest St. Louis County is renowned for its high property values, quality schools, and over 300 acres of parkland. Its historic downtown district is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and remains a focal point of community life (City of Kirkwood).

attractions.name: Citygarden
attractions.description: Citygarden is a vibrant urban oasis in downtown St. Louis that spans 2.9 acres and features modern and contemporary sculptures set against lush landscaping and interactive water features (Citygarden, TCLF). The park is designed without fences or walls, inviting visitors to interact with the art and enjoy a serene blending of nature and culture in the heart of the city (Citygarden, City of St. Louis).
attractions.hours: Sunrise to 10 pm daily (Citygarden)
attractions.admission: Free (Citygarden)
highlights: Over two dozen works of sculpture by world-renowned artists such as Keith Haring and Fernand Léger (Wikipedia)
The Split Basin, a granite and limestone water feature mimicking Missouri waterfalls (Citygarden)
The Children's Spray Plaza, featuring 102 night-lit vertical jets for seasonal play (TCLF)
A 550-foot long arcing Missouri limestone wall that divides the park's landscape (Kids Out and About)
The serpentine 'meander wall' capped in polished granite, providing ample seating for visitors (City of St. Louis)
An educational audio tour available via the official website to enhance the connection to the garden's sculptures (Citygarden)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to touch and interact with the many sculptures throughout the park (TCLF). Located on the banks of the Mississippi River, the 331-acre site is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and features numerous monuments honoring the sacrifices of American service members from every major conflict in the nation's history. (VA.gov, Wikipedia)

Dining & Shopping: A variety of local favorites like Square One Brewery, Clementine’s Creamery, and Polite Society.
Community Events: Hosts regular concerts, a holiday marketplace, and neighborhood festivals throughout the year.
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes to enjoy the historic architecture and park trails. Check the event calendar for free summer concerts in the park. Parking is best found on the streets surrounding the park, such as Park Avenue.

attractions.name: St. Louis Walk of Fame
attractions.description: The St. Louis Walk of Fame honors notable St. Louisans who have made significant national contributions to American culture and history. Established in 1988 by Joe Edwards, it features brass stars and bronze biographical plaques embedded in the sidewalks of the Delmar Loop. The attraction spans several blocks, celebrating achievements in fields ranging from music and literature to science and sports.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Monday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Over 150 brass stars and bronze biographical plaques honoring famous St. Louisans
Located in the historic Delmar Loop, designated as one of the '10 Great Streets in America'
Features stars for legends like Chuck Berry, Maya Angelou, Tina Turner, and Jon Hamm
Biographical plaques provide educational insights into each inductee's national impact
Includes a 7-foot tall bronze statue of rock & roll pioneer Chuck Berry nearby
The Walk is open to the public 24/7 as an outdoor sidewalk attraction
attractions.tips: The stars are located along the 6000-6600 blocks of Delmar Blvd; start your walk near the Chuck Berry statue for a central experience. Use the MetroLink light rail to the Delmar Loop station for easy access without worrying about street parking. Visit at night to see the area's vibrant neon signs and lit-up storefronts while exploring the stars. Bring comfortable walking shoes as the Walk spans several city blocks.

attractions.name: Ted Drewes Frozen Custard
attractions.description: Ted Drewes Frozen Custard is a legendary family-owned dessert destination in St. Louis, Missouri, established in 1929 by Ted Drewes, Sr. A staple of the historic Route 66, it is world-renowned for its signature "concrete" treats—frozen custard so dense and creamy that it is famously served upside down.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM

highlights: Famous for its 'Concrete,' a frozen custard treat so thick it won't fall out when held upside down.
Landmark location situated on the historic Mother Road, Route 66.
Family-owned business spanning four generations of tradition and service since 1929.
Offers a wide variety of over 30 toppings and signature specialty flavors like the Cardinal Sin.
Features a gift shop and historical museum across from the main custard stand.
Recognized by national media and celebrity chefs as a quintessential St. Louis experience.

attractions.name: The Delmar Loop (The Delmar Loop)
attractions.description: The Delmar Loop is a vibrant six-block entertainment, cultural, and restaurant district spanning University City and the western edge of St. Louis. Named "One of the 10 Great Streets in America" by the American Planning Association, it features an eclectic mix of over 140 specialty shops, world-class music venues, and diverse international dining options. The neighborhood is famous for its rich musical heritage and the iconic St. Louis Walk of Fame. (The Delmar Loop, Explore St. Louis)

Originally built to attract an NFL team, it served as the home of the St. Louis Rams for two decades and now hosts the St. Louis Battlehawks. The venue is a premier destination for major concerts, trade shows, and sporting events, offering versatile configurations that can accommodate over 80,000 guests (Wikipedia).

Established by immigrants attracted to local clay mines in the late 19th century, it remains one of the city's most vibrant and culturally rich communities today. (The Hill St. Louis, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Tower Grove Park
attractions.description: Tower Grove Park is a public park and arboretum in St. Louis, Missouri, designated as a National Historic Landmark. Gifted to the city by philanthropist Henry Shaw in 1868, it is celebrated as the largest and best-preserved 19th-century Gardenesque style city park in the United States. The 289-acre urban oasis features nearly 7,000 trees, 32 historic pavilions, and a diverse range of recreational and cultural programs. (Tower Grove Park, Wikipedia)

Tower Grove Farmers' Market, a popular weekly event held Saturday mornings (Official Website)
Henry Shaw Memorial Tennis Center, featuring some of the only public grass courts in the nation (Official Website)
Piper Palm House, a historic conservatory and the oldest greenhouse west of the Mississippi (Expedia)
attractions.tips: Visit on Saturday mornings for the Farmers' Market to experience local food and crafts; use the GPS-enabled interactive map available on the official website to explore the vast grounds; and check the event calendar for free community concerts and festivals. highlights: Two stocked fishing lakes (North and South Lakes) known for bass and bluegill.
The 'Playground for Everybody,' an inclusive playground designed for children of all abilities.
Southwest City Dog Park, a premier 1.1-acre fenced off-leash area for members.
An 18-hole disc golf course and six permanent pickleball courts with dedicated nets.
Extensive sports facilities including tennis courts, softball fields, and a roller hockey rink.
Multiple covered picnic pavilions available for reservation.
attractions.tips: While the park's official address is on Hampton Ave, the only vehicle entrance is located just south of the intersection of Hampton and Jamieson Avenues. Additional street parking is available along Jamieson Avenue for easier access to the playground area. The North Lake is widely considered the better spot for local fishermen looking for bass. TripAdvisor, City of St. Louis
